articles.title: MINUTE READ)
articles.summary: While Google may be behind a bit in the AI game, they are leaning hard into their advantage of all-the-eyeballs-in-the-universe. The feedback loop on making sure results are positive is tight and depending on how they utilize it, could be a differentiator. Don’t count the GOOG out just yet.

articles.title: GLOBAL VENTURE FUNDING IN Q3 2023 FALLS AGAIN
articles.summary: A great recap of Q3 funding across a variety of sectors. The short version: things are down except for the hot spaces and no end in sight for the IPO market slump.

articles.title: THE HYPER-PERSONALIZATION OF EVERYTHING
articles.summary: A detailed take on how AI is going to impact the digital world as it relates to the individual consumer. Just as in media, hyper personalization makes it tough to build (or maintain) a juggernaut but also opens up so many opportunities for niche brands that can find their users.

articles.title: HUBSPOT MARKETING VS. KLAVIYO: TWO TITANS, TWO PATHS
articles.summary: With so much of their data out in the open as public companies, it’s hard not to compare and contrast these two behemoths. One is focused on e-commerce, while the other takes a broader approach. They are both growing at crazy rates.

articles.title: THE THIN LINE BETWEEN BOLD AND RECKLESS
articles.summary: This article explores the Vanderbilt of yesteryear and asks whether we could possibly be as reckless again today. History always repeats itself and looking at it, especially with a lens on the current technology cycle, always yields helpful insights.

articles.title: FOUNDERS
articles.summary: This article is a deep dive into funding options outside of equity. It is the first part of a multi-part series that covers debt options for startups. It is a great primer for those new to (or revisiting) the topic.

articles.title: DATALENS
articles.summary: A modern business intelligence and data visualization system.

articles.title: WHEELS OF JUSTICE SLOW TO ACCEPT LEGAL TECH AS FUNDING FALLS
articles.summary: Even with the onslaught of AI company funding in the last year, legal tech has slowed significantly. The view from the trenches is there is a lot of noise out there with new companies appearing regularly but not a lot of meat on the bones or sales on the books just yet. It will be curious to see if this is a trend or just a blip.

articles.title: OPEN SOURCE AI BUSINESS MODELS & BRAND MOATS
articles.summary: We’ve seen how open source models can work and thrive over the last 25+ years and even as they mature and become more “closed” (*cough* Redhat *cough*), the model has lasting impacts. In the face of AI, not all playbooks are the same. This article provides a thoughtful and in-depth review of open source models and how AI will play in this.

articles.title: GIVING AND RECEIVING ADVICE
articles.summary: Giving and receiving advice is fraught with complexities due to varying motivations and the presence of ambivalence in advice-seekers. It's vital to navigate advice with discernment, acknowledging that valuable guidance can sometimes come from unexpected sources.

articles.title: WHAT DO VENTURE CAPITALISTS DO?
articles.summary: A nostalgic look at a research paper from 1984 around what VCs do all day and a view on how all of that has changed. And boy, has it changed.

articles.title: VENTURE CAPITAL TERM SHEET: WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW
articles.summary: When a venture fund wants to invest in a startup, it sends the founders a term sheet. As the name suggests, this short document details the terms and conditions upon which the fund intends to invest.

articles.title: MEDIOCRE SUCCESS IS WORSE THAN OUTRIGHT FAILURE
articles.summary: For startups, ambiguous "mediocre successes" in experiments are more detrimental than clear failures, as they hinder decisive learning and growth.
